findings.title: Hardcoded secret in configuration
findings.problem: API secret is hardcoded directly in the source code.
findings.action: Move the secret to an environment variable or secret manager.

findings.title: SQL injection in user query
findings.problem: User input is concatenated into a SQL query without sanitization.
findings.suggested_fix: db.Query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", id)

findings.title: Error from ValidateToken is ignored
findings.problem: The error returned by ValidateToken() is discarded.
findings.action: Check the error and return HTTP 401 on failure.

fix_options.approach: Return 401 immediately on any validation error.
fix_options.cons: No distinction between expired and malformed tokens.
fix_options.risk_if_skipped: Invalid tokens are treated as valid.

fix_options.approach: Branch on the error type to return 401 or 400.

findings.recommendation_reasoning: Fail-closed is the safer default for auth.

findings.title: Consider a table-driven test
findings.problem: The three near-identical test functions could be table-driven.
findings.action: Collapse the cases into a single table-driven test.

summary: The change introduces authentication but ships a hardcoded secret and an SQL injection that must be fixed before merge.
recommendation: Do not merge until the BLOCKING and CRITICAL findings are resolved.
